metadata_frame: The following question is drawn from Will Socialism Benefit the English People?, a debate published in 1884 by Henry Hyndman and Charles Bradlaugh, a British politician.
main_question: It is arguable that the transition to, and maintenance of, a Socialist State will require not just a political revolution, but also a mental revolution. How might a politician who is pro-socialist define what a mental revolution means?
answer_strings: It is a reflexion of the revolution that is going on below in the forms of production to-day.

A revolution in which you alter all present forms of expression – a revolution in which you efface the habit of centuries of education – a revolution in which the use of the words “my house,” “my coat,” “my watch,” “my book,” all disappear.
It is a rapid, large-scale shift in the cognitive and interpretive frameworks through which people understand social or natural reality.

A profound change in the prevailing habits of thought, moral assumptions, and intellectual outlook of a society, through which people come to understand the world and their place in it in fundamentally new ways.
source_title: Will Socialism Benefit the English People?

metadata_frame: The following question is drawn from Will Socialism Benefit the English People?, a debate published in 1884 by Henry Hyndman and Charles Bradlaugh, a British politician.
main_question: It is arguable that the transition to, and maintenance of, a Socialist State will require not just a political revolution, but also a mental revolution. How might a politician who is anti-socialist describe what a mental revolution means?
answer_strings: A revolution in which you alter all present forms of expression – a revolution in which you efface the habit of centuries of education – a revolution in which the use of the words “my house,” “my coat,” “my watch,” “my book,” all disappear.
It is a reflexion of the revolution that is going on below in the forms of production to-day.
It is a rapid, large-scale shift in the cognitive and interpretive frameworks through which people understand social or natural reality.

A profound change in the prevailing habits of thought, moral assumptions, and intellectual outlook of a society, through which people come to understand the world and their place in it in fundamentally new ways.
source_title: Will Socialism Benefit the English People?

metadata_frame: The following question is drawn from Will Socialism Benefit the English People?, a debate published in 1884 by Henry Hyndman and Charles Bradlaugh, a British politician.
main_question: If asked the question, What is the role of the individual in advancing civilisation?, how might a politicians who is anti-socialist answer?
answer_strings: Civilisation has only been in proportion to the energy and enterprise of the individual.
All the great advances have been made by men, even under our present individual system, who were really imbued with the collective idea.
The development of civilization depends less upon the isolated genius of individuals than upon the structural conditions of society that make discovery, invention, and collaboration possible.
History shows that the greatest advances in civilization come when individuals are given the freedom and opportunity to act on their ideas.
source_title: Will Socialism Benefit the English People?

metadata_frame: The following question is drawn from Will Socialism Benefit the English People?, a debate published in 1884 by Henry Hyndman and Charles Bradlaugh, a British politician.
main_question: If asked the question, What is the role of the individual in advancing civilisation?, how might a politicians who is pro-socialist answer?
answer_strings: All the great advances have been made by men, even under our present individual system, who were really imbued with the collective idea.
Civilisation has only been in proportion to the energy and enterprise of the individual.
Even within our present individualistic system, the great advances have typically come from individuals who recognized the importance of the broader social good.
Even in a society organized around individual achievement, the most important breakthroughs tend to arise from people working toward common objectives.
source_title: Will Socialism Benefit the English People?
